sidebar.noServers: No servers configured — open Settings › Servers

serverDelete.title: Remove server?
serverDelete.message: Server "{name}" will be removed here. Sessions will keep running — they just stop appearing on the dashboard.

flows.empty: No flows yet — create the first one

projects.pageSubtitle: Manage your projects — servers, groups, terminals, notes, flows and prompts live inside a project.
projects.emptyDescription: No servers, groups, terminals, notes, flows or prompts.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete project
deleteConfirm.message: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"?

deleteBlocked.title: Cannot delete
deleteBlocked.instructions: Move or delete these items before deleting the project.

groupSelector.openAllEditors: Open VS Code for every session in this group
groupSelector.openAllBlocked: {blocked} of {total} openings blocked by browser. Allow popups for this site in your settings.
groupSelector.openAllRemoteStaggerHint: Staggered open (1.5s between each). If folders overwrite each other instead of opening multiple windows, enable "window.openFoldersInNewWindow": "on" in VS Code.

newTerminal.title: New Terminal
newTerminal.noServers: No servers configured. Go to Settings › Servers.

browser.truncated: Showing {n} of {total}+ folders — type a path manually for direct access
browser.serverOffline: Server unreachable — type a path manually

capture.title: Terminal capture
capture.copyFailed: Clipboard unavailable — selection made, press Ctrl/Cmd+C
capture.hint: Plain-text snapshot of the pane's scrollback. Select freely with mouse / touch and copy with Ctrl/Cmd+C.

mosaic.emptyState: Click a session in the sidebar to begin

clipboard.title: Clipboard
clipboard.sendSubtitle: Pick a terminal — Claude Code recognizes @path as attachment
clipboard.limitReached: Limit of {max} images — delete some before adding more

errors.group_name_too_long: Group name must be at most {max} characters
errors.group_name_taken: A group with this name already exists
errors.prompt_name_too_long: Prompt name must be at most {max} characters
errors.prompt_body_too_long: Prompt body must be at most {max} characters
errors.note_title_too_long: Title must be at most {max} characters
errors.note_content_too_long: Content must be at most {max} characters
errors.flow_name_too_long: Flow name must be at most {max} characters
errors.project_name_too_long: Project name must be at most {max} characters
errors.project_name_taken: A project with this name already exists
errors.project_default_protected: The default project cannot be deleted
errors.editor_binary_not_found: Editor binary not found on the host. Install VSCode / Cursor / VSCodium, or set the path in Settings → Editor.
errors.remote_editor_no_target: Server has no host/SSH alias configured. Edit the server in Settings → Servers.

storage.unavailable: Storage is unavailable. Check the remote connection.
storage.reload_failed: Storage reload failed: {reason}. The config file was saved but activation failed; fix the issue or remove the config.

s3.access_key_required: S3 Access Key ID is required.
s3.secret_key_required: S3 Secret Access Key is required.
s3.bucket_not_found: S3 bucket not found or not accessible.

storage.config_already_absent: No remote storage config to remove.
storage.sync_local_to_cloud: Local data pushed to cloud ({count} file(s)).
storage.sync_cloud_to_local: Cloud data pulled to local files ({count} file(s)).

settings.selectServerHint: Add a server in the Servers tab first.

editor.title: Editor
editor.subtitle: Choose which editor opens when you click 'Open in VSCode' on a session. Leave blank to auto-detect.
editor.pathHint: Absolute path to the CLI binary. Works with VSCode, Cursor, VSCodium, Windsurf — anything that accepts a folder path as argument.
editor.clearOverride: Clear the override and fall back to auto-detect

source.fallback: Found at a well-known install location.

servers.title: Servers
servers.subtitle: Each server runs a Pulse client with its own API key.
servers.empty: No servers registered — click Add to get started.
servers.sshAliasHint: If set, the "open remote editor" button uses this alias from your ~/.ssh/config instead of the IP. Required when the server uses a custom SSH key (ssh -i).
servers.saveBlocked: Couldn't connect: {reason}. Fix the values or force save.
servers.deleteConfirmMessage: Sessions will keep running — they'll just stop appearing here.

reason.mixed_content: Mixed content (HTTP server on an HTTPS dashboard)

notifications.title: Telegram Notifications
notifications.subtitle: Get a Telegram message when a monitored session stays idle for a while
notifications.botTokenHint: Create a bot via @BotFather on Telegram and paste the token here
notifications.chatIdHint: Send any message to your bot on Telegram and click Detect — the client will fetch it via getUpdates
notifications.detectChatId: Detect chat ID from recent messages to the bot
notifications.timeoutHint: A monitored session is considered idle after this many seconds without activity. Min 15, max 3600.
notifications.telegramNotConfigured: Set up Telegram in the matching tab to enable this channel.

storage.title: Data storage
storage.subtitle: Local files by default. Point to a remote driver (MongoDB, S3 or S3-compatible) to share your data across multiple installations — hot-swap, no restart.
storage.syncSubtitle: One-way copy between local files and the active cloud driver. Both operations are destructive on the target side.

file.label: Local files
file.description: The default. Everything lives in data/*.json on this machine.

mongo.label: MongoDB
mongo.description: Point at a MongoDB and every API route reads/writes through it. Requires MongoDB 4.2+.
mongo.uriHint: mongodb:// or mongodb+srv:// URI including credentials

s3.label: S3
s3.description: AWS S3 or any S3-compatible endpoint (R2, GCS, MinIO, Backblaze B2). Uses ETag If-Match for cross-device write safety.
s3.endpointHint: Leave empty for AWS S3. For R2/GCS/MinIO provide the full URL, e.g. https://<account>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com
s3.regionHint: Defaults to us-east-1. Use "auto" for Cloudflare R2.
s3.prefixHint: Path prefix inside the bucket. Useful when sharing the bucket with other apps.
s3.forcePathStyleLabel: Use path-style addressing (required for MinIO and some self-hosted S3)

confirmPush.title: Replace cloud contents
confirmPush.body: This will WIPE the active cloud storage and replace it with the current local files. Local files are not changed.

confirmPull.title: Overwrite local files
confirmPull.body: This will OVERWRITE the local files in data/ with the current contents of the active cloud storage. The cloud is not changed.

confirmDeactivate.title: Deactivate remote storage
confirmDeactivate.body: The frontend will go back to reading local files in data/ — the files that were there before you activated remote storage are still intact. If you want the *current* cloud state preserved locally, optionally run Sync cloud → local first; otherwise this is a clean rollback to the pre-activation state.

prompts.pageSubtitle: Save snippets and send them to any terminal
prompts.makeGlobalHelp: Available in every project (not tied to the active one)
prompts.empty: No prompts yet — create the first one

notifications.browserSectionHint: Get native browser notifications when a monitored session goes idle. Runs in parallel with Telegram.
notifications.permissionDenied: Blocked — enable in your browser settings
notifications.permissionDeniedToast: Permission denied — only Telegram will notify
notifications.insecureContextToast: Native notifications need HTTPS or localhost — you're on {origin}. Telegram still works.
notifications.insecureContextBannerBody: Browsers block the Notifications API on non-secure origins. Pulse is served over HTTP at {origin}, so the bell-icon toasts won't be able to show desktop notifications. Options: open the dashboard from http://localhost:{port} on this machine, set up HTTPS with a reverse proxy, or rely on Telegram (configured under Settings → Telegram).
notifications.muteSoundHint: Do not play a sound when a notification arrives.
notifications.presencePolicyLabel: When to consider this terminal visible
notifications.presencePolicyStrictHint: Requires a visible tab, focused window, and recent activity.
notifications.presencePolicyVisibleHint: A visible tab and on-screen terminal are enough, even without focus.
notifications.presencePolicyNote: Browsers cannot know which monitor you are physically watching; they only expose window focus, tab visibility, and viewport.
notifications.unsupported: Your browser does not support native notifications

confirmDelete.title: Delete note?
confirmDelete.message: The note will be permanently removed.

modal.title: Update available — Pulse v{version}
modal.subtitle: {count} server(s) need updating
modal.instructions: Open your terminal (on Windows: WSL terminal) and run the command below on each server's machine:

acceptModal.title: Server connection issue
acceptModal.subtitle: {count} server(s) need attention

tlsSection.title: Accept self-signed certificate
tlsSection.intro: These HTTPS servers use a self-signed certificate. Open the link below in a new tab and accept the warning ("Advanced" → "Continue anyway"), then come back and click Re-test.

mixedSection.title: Mixed content
mixedSection.intro: These servers are configured with HTTP, but the dashboard is running over HTTPS. The browser blocks these requests. Edit each server and switch the protocol to HTTPS.

acceptModal.silence: Don't warn me about this server
acceptModal.silencedToast: Server silenced. Re-enable in Settings → Servers.
